# BUG: moving the iota anchor (the spec carrying `= iota`) strands sibling
# specs in the source const block — they lose their initialiser and produce
# `missing init expr for X` build errors.
#
# Hit while splitting internal/infer/infer.go in the gojure project: the
# resolver moved `OriginUnknown Origin = iota` alone via `-receiver Origin`
# and the four sibling specs in the source block became `const ( Annotated
# … )` without a type/iota anchor — invalid Go.
#
# Expected fix: when moving a spec that carries the `= iota` initialiser
# from a grouped const block, sflit must either (a) refuse and emit a
# diagnostic, or (b) hoist the initialiser onto the next remaining spec so
# the chain survives in the source. Option (b) preserves valid Go without
# operator intervention.

exec sflit -source big.go -sink small.go -regex '^KindUnknown$' -move
cmp big.go expected_big.go
cmp small.go expected_small.go

-- big.go --
package p

type Kind int

const (
	KindUnknown Kind = iota
	KindA
	KindB
)
-- expected_big.go --
package p

type Kind int

const (
	KindA Kind = iota
	KindB
)
-- expected_small.go --
package p

const KindUnknown Kind = 0
